details: Strongest NTL deviation in the Czech dataset (Z-score = -4.52). Sustained suppressed industrial lighting through peak summer production period â€” extremely anomalous for automotive manufacturing zones.

economic_intelligence.what: VIIRS DNB radiance at 5-year minimum for the site. Z-score -4.52 is the most extreme temporal deviation in the entire Czechia NTL dataset.
economic_intelligence.where: Mládá Boleslav, Bohemia â€” Škoda Auto MladĂˇ Boleslav Plant, producing Octavia, Fabia, Karoq models.
economic_intelligence.when: July 2023. Production traditionally peaks in summer before August holiday shutdown; July anomaly indicates exceptional disruption.
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score -4.52 â€” 1-in-1,000,000 event under normal distribution. Only 0.0003% probability under null hypothesis.
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: VW Group issued profit warning July 2023 (-15% EPS guidance). Czech GDP growth revised down to 0.3% for 2023 (CNB forecast). Škoda factory confirmed 2-week partial shutdown.
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: Volkswagen Group Q2 2023 earnings call (July 2023); Czech National Bank (CNB) GDP nowcast Q3 2023; Bloomberg terminal automotive sector analytics.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: VIIRS sensor degradation could reduce apparent radiance; however, cross-validation with adjacent Liberec industrial zone (stable NTL) eliminates sensor artifact hypothesis.

verification.ground_truth: Czech Statistical Office (CSÚ) Quarterly Manufacturing Index
